evals.prompt: Build a slide outline for a journal club presentation on a paper about waterborne epoxy modified emulsified asphalt. The paper has: an introduction about tack coat bonding problems, a materials section with emulsion formulation, results showing 28% bonding strength improvement, FTIR mechanism evidence, and a conclusion about engineering potential.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must produce a slide outline that follows the logical evidence-chain structure (background → problem → method → results → mechanism → discussion → conclusion), not manuscript section order. The outline should follow the journal club deck sequence: title + why it matters, engineering problem, material system and design idea, experiment matrix, key results, mechanism evidence, limitations, takeaways, discussion questions. Each slide must have one purpose. Figures should only be placed where they prove the slide claim.

assertions.name: evidence_chain_structure
assertions.description: The slide outline follows the evidence chain (background → problem → method → results → mechanism → discussion → conclusion), not manuscript section order.

assertions.name: journal_club_sequence
assertions.description: The outline includes the journal club deck elements: title/why it matters, engineering problem, material system, experiment matrix, key results, mechanism evidence, limitations, takeaways, discussion questions.

assertions.name: one_purpose_per_slide
assertions.description: Each slide has a single defined purpose.

assertions.name: figures_tied_to_claims
assertions.description: Figures are placed only where they directly support the slide claim.

evals.prompt: I have a reader package from materials-reader for a paper on emulsified asphalt bonding. The package includes figure_handoff references for Figure 3 (bonding strength bar chart) and Figure 5 (FTIR spectra). Build a slide outline that preserves these figure handoffs.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must preserve the figure_handoff references from the reader package. Figure 3 (bonding strength) should be placed on the key results slide(s), and Figure 5 (FTIR spectra) should be placed on the mechanism evidence slide. The assistant must not drop or relocate figures to slides where they don't support the claim. The slide outline should reference the reader_anchor for each figure.

assertions.name: figure_handoffs_preserved
assertions.description: All figure_handoff references from the reader package are preserved in the slide outline.

assertions.name: figures_placed_correctly
assertions.description: Performance figures (bonding strength) are placed on results slides; characterization figures (FTIR) are placed on mechanism slides.

assertions.name: reader_anchors_referenced
assertions.description: The slide outline references reader_anchor for each figure.

evals.prompt: 为这篇关于水性环氧改性乳化沥青的论文做一个中文组会 PPT 大纲。论文摘要：'Waterborne epoxy resin (WER) was used to modify emulsified asphalt (EA). The bonding strength increased by 28% at 10% WER dosage. FTIR confirmed the epoxy-amine curing reaction. The material shows potential for tack coat applications.'
evals.expected_output: The assistant must produce slide titles and takeaways in Chinese by default (per the skill gate: 'Use Chinese slide titles by default, English only when requested'). Key technical terms like 'waterborne epoxy', 'emulsified asphalt', 'tack coat', 'FTIR', 'bonding strength' should be preserved in English alongside Chinese. The outline should follow the journal-club deck structure. The presentation thesis should be one sentence summarizing the paper's contribution.

assertions.name: chinese_titles_default
assertions.description: Slide titles and takeaways are in Chinese by default.

assertions.name: key_terms_preserved_in_english
assertions.description: Key technical terms (waterborne epoxy, emulsified asphalt, tack coat, FTIR, bonding strength) are preserved in English alongside Chinese.

assertions.name: presentation_thesis_present
assertions.description: The outline includes a one-sentence presentation thesis summarizing the paper's contribution.

evals.prompt: Build a slide deck outline for a 15-minute group meeting presentation on a 12-page materials science paper. The paper covers: introduction, materials and methods (3 formulations), results (bonding strength, FTIR, SEM, thermal analysis), discussion, and conclusion.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must produce a slide count appropriate for 15 minutes: roughly 8-12 slides (allowing ~1-1.5 minutes per slide). The assistant should not include every figure from the paper — only the 3-6 most important evidence points. The outline should prioritize key results and mechanism evidence over exhaustive methods detail. If the assistant produces more than 15 slides, it should flag that the presentation may exceed the time limit.

assertions.name: slide_count_appropriate
assertions.description: The slide count is appropriate for a 15-minute presentation (roughly 8-12 slides, allowing ~1-1.5 minutes per slide).

assertions.name: evidence_points_prioritized
assertions.description: The outline selects 3-6 key evidence points rather than including every figure from the paper.

assertions.name: methods_not_exhaustive
assertions.description: The outline does not include exhaustive methods detail; it prioritizes key results and mechanism evidence.
